task_context.question: What is the setting for the game engine Snowdrop?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: What is the setting for the game engine Snowdrop?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
First-hop evidence: Snowdrop (game engine) — "for use on Microsoft Windows, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One."  
Bridge needed: Confirm that Snowdrop officially supports the listed platforms (Microsoft Windows; Nintendo Switch; PlayStation 4; Xbox One).  
Retrieval direction: 1. Fetch the page titled "Snowdrop (game engine)" (Wikipedia) and inspect the lead and infobox for 'Platforms'/'Supported platforms'. 2. Fetch Ubisoft's official Snowdrop engine page (or search "Snowdrop game engine site:ubisoft.com") and check 'Supported platforms' or technical/specs sections.

current_output.query:
1) Fetch the Wikipedia page:
GET https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Snowdrop_(game_engine)
- Extract the lead paragraph and the infobox "Platforms" / "Supported platforms" field (copy exact text).

2) Fetch Ubisoft's official Snowdrop information:
a) Try direct Ubisoft engine URL:
GET https://www.ubisoft.com/en-us/engine/snowdrop
b) If (a) not available, try:
GET https://snowdrop.ubisoft.com
c) If direct pages fail, run a site-restricted search:
Search query: site:ubisoft.com "Snowdrop" "engine" "platform" OR "supported"
- From any Ubisoft page returned, extract the supported platforms list or technical/spec statement and copy the exact text/snippet and URL.

3) If either source lists platforms, compare against the list: Microsoft Windows; Nintendo Switch; PlayStation 4; Xbox One. Return the confirmed list and cite the source(s) and the exact text/snippets used for confirmation.

output.query: Tom Clancy's The Division setting near-future New York City smallpox pandemic
Tom Clancy's The Division What is the setting for the game engine Snowdrop?

retrieved_titles: Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six: Shadow Vanguard

retrieved_doc_snippets: Snowdrop (game engine) | Snowdrop is a proprietary game engine created by Ubisoft for use on Microsoft Windows, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One. It was first revealed at E3 2013 with "Tom Clancy's The Division", the first game using the engine.

Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell (video game) | Tom Clancy's Splinter Cell is a 2002 stealth video game developed by Ubi Soft Montreal and built on the Unreal Engine 2. It is the first "Splinter Cell" game in the series. Endorsed by author Tom Clancy, it follows the activities of NSA black ops agent Sam Fisher. The character of Fisher is voiced by actor Michael Ironside.

Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six: Shadow Vanguard | Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six: Shadow Vanguard is a 2011 first-person shooter developed and published by Gameloft for iOS, Xperia Play and Android devices. The game is set within the continuity of "Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six" media franchise.

Tom Clancy's The Division | Tom Clancy's The Division is an online-only action role-playing video game developed by Massive Entertainment and published by Ubisoft, with assistance from Red Storm Entertainment, for Microsoft Windows, PlayStation 4 and Xbox One. It was announced during Ubisoft's E3 2013 press conference, and was released worldwide on 8 March 2016. It is set in a near future New York City in the aftermath of a smallpox pandemic; the player, who is an agent of the eponymous Strategi ...[truncated]

Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon: Future Soldier | Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon: Future Soldier is a third-person tactical shooter video game developed and published by Ubisoft for the PlayStation 3, Xbox 360 and Microsoft Windows. It was released in May and June 2012. "Tom Clancy's Ghost Recon: Future Soldier" was announced to be in development by Ubisoft on January 22, 2009. The game has a futuristic take on the "Ghost Recon" series. The campaign has settings such as Nigeria, Pakistan, Russia, and Norway.

Tom Clancy's | Tom Clancy's is a branding used by video game company Ubisoft for video games featuring the works of American author Tom Clancy (while a majority of the games are unrelated to Clancy's work).

Tom Clancy's Rainbow 6: Patriots | Tom Clancy's Rainbow 6: Patriots is a cancelled first-person shooter video game, part of the "Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six" series, announced on the cover of the December 2011 issue of "Game Informer". It was to be published by Ubisoft, and was developed by the company's Montreal studio, with additional development by Ubisoft Toronto and Red Storm Entertainment. Due to the death of Tom Clancy in October 2013, concern was raised that this game would become the last  ...[truncated]
target_state.summary_2: Snowdrop is a proprietary game engine created by Ubisoft "for use on Microsoft Windows, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One." (Snowdrop (game engine))
target_state.answer: Microsoft Windows, Nintendo Switch, PlayStation 4, and Xbox One
